revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:東屋沼神社]] {{Expand Japanese|東屋沼神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Azumayanuma Shrine | native_name = 東屋沼神社 | image = Haiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Azumayanuma Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.805485|140.442935}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} {{nihongo|'''Azumayanuma Shrine'''|東屋沼神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 31 ** P155: {{ill|Q17228379|ltq=Q17228379|qid=Q17228379|1=Kuronuma Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039997|ltq=Q135039997|qid=Q135039997|1=Atsumayakunino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Fukushima (city)|lt=Fukushima|an|Fukushima|ar|فوكوشيما (مدينة)|arz|فوكوشيما (تجمع سكان فى فوكوشيما)|azb|فوکوشیما (شهر)|az|Fukuşima|ban|Fukushima, Fukushima|bcl|Fukushima, Fukushima|be|Фукусіма (горад)|br|Fukushima (kêr)|ca|Fukushima (ciutat)|ceb|Fukushima-shi (kapital sa prepektura sa Hapon)|ce|Фукусима (гӀала)|ckb|فووکووشیما|cs|Fukušima|de|Fukushima (Fukushima)|el|Φουκουσίμα (πόλη)|en|Fukushima (city)|envoyage|Fukushima|eo|Fukuŝimo|es|Fukushima (Fukushima)|et|Fukushima|eu|Fukushima|fa|فوکوشیما (شهر)|fi|Fukushima|frr|Fukushima|fr|Fukushima (ville de la préfecture de Fukushima)|frvoyage|Fukushima|ga|Fukushima|gl|Fukushima|he|פוקושימה|hr|Fukushima|hu|Fukusima|hy|Ֆուկուսիմա (քաղաք)|id|Fukushima, Fukushima|is|Fukushima|it|Fukushima|itvoyage|Fukushima|ja|福島市|javoyage|福島市|kn|ಫುಕುಷಿಮಾ|ko|후쿠시마시|ku|Fukushima|lt|Fukušima|lv|Fukusima|mg|Fukushima|mk|Фукушима|ml|ഫുക്കുഷിമ (город)|scn|Fukushima|sc|Fukushima|sh|Fukushima|simple|Fukushima (city)|sl|Fukušima, Fukušima|sq|Fukushima|sr|Фукушима|sv|Fukushima|sw|Fukushima, Fukushima|ta|புகுசிமா|tg|Фукушима|th|ฟูกูชิมะ (เมือง)|tl|Lungsod ng Fukushima|tr|Fukuşima|tt|Фукусима (шәһәр)|tw|Fukushima|uk|Фукусіма (Фукусіма)|ur|فوکوشیما|uz|Fukusima|vi|Fukushima (thành phố)|war|Fukushima (syudad)|wuu|福岛市|za|Fuzdauj Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q3275687|ltq=Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20151121015632/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/276002.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182068 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8380005000636 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県福島市飯坂町平野字明神脇1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:東屋沼神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11526673}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Azumayanuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|270px|Haiden of Azumayanuma Shrine]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fukushima City|Q161176|qid=Q161176|1=Fukushima (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirano, Iizaka-machi|Q11666149|qid=Q11666149|1=iizakamachihirano}} Aza Myōjinwaki 1 |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|48|19.69|N|140|26|35.10|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Azumayanuma Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto)}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Dai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, later {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |Other names = Azumayaharu Shrine, Nana-ō Daimyōjin, Nanamatsu Daimyōjin |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Azumayanuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Azumayanuma Shrine''' (あずまやぬまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fukushima City|Q161176|qid=Q161176|1=Fukushima (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirano, Iizaka-machi|Q11666149|qid=Q11666149|1=iizakamachihirano}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was first classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} and later as a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami}}''' * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt='''Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto''' (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto)}} * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}''' === History === According to the shrine’s origin record, the date of founding is unknown. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists it as “{{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Shinobu District|Q11386050|qid=Q11386050|1=Shinobu district}} Azumayanuma Shrine, Myōjin Dai.” Among the five {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} enshrined in Shinobu District (Azumayanuma Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumayakuni Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shirawase Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Kuronuma Shrine|Q17228379|qid=Q17228379|1=Kuronuma Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Kashima Shrine (Oda, Fukushima City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashima Shrine}}), it alone is designated as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It was also a site where the state ritual “Myōjin Festival” was conducted. Before the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was regarded as the head shrine of the more than twenty villages in the Amari district located between the {{ill|lt=Surikamigawa River|Q11498645|qid=Q11498645|1=Surikami River}} and the {{ill|lt=Matsukawa River (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11530259|qid=Q11530259|lt=Matsukawa|1=Matsukawa}} flowing through present-day Fukushima City. At the time of its founding, the shrine buildings were located near Kaminuma (Thunder Pond) on {{ill|lt=Mount Azuma|Q2669374|qid=Q2669374|1=Mount Azuma}}. They were later relocated to Kioroshi-yama (Sacred Forest Mountain) in the {{ill|lt=Ōzaso|Q11438455|qid=Q11438455|1=Ozaso}} area at the foot of the mountain, and subsequently moved again to the present site. In ancient times, the shrine was known by various names, the main ones being as follows: ; “Nana-ō Daimyōjin” : When the shrine was relocated to the Ōzaso area, seven targets were hung on trees within the shrine grounds for archery practice. ; “Nanamatsu Daimyōjin” : Seven pine trees at the shrine were said to be inhabited by snakes that exterminated mice; these snakes were revered as messengers of the gods. ; “Azumayaharu Shrine” : In the 10th year of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An’ei|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|qid=Q7719|1=1781}}, the name was given with the meaning that “through divine protection, the governance of the eastern lands (Azumaya) is brought to order.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji 2|Q11512277|qid=Q11512277|1=Meiji – year 2}}, the shrine reverted to its present name, “Azumayanuma Shrine.” The museum within the shrine grounds preserves ridge tags, dedicatory plaques, photographs, and other materials from the periods when it was known as “Nana-ō Daimyōjin,” “Nanamatsu Daimyōjin,” and “Azumayaharu Shrine.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|qid=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 8 it became a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Today, it is chiefly worshipped as a deity of abundant harvests, protection from calamities, and fire prevention. === Grounds === === Shrine buildings === The shrine complex consists of a haiden (worship hall), heiden (offering hall), and honden (main sanctuary). File:Right side of Honden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden
Irimoya-zukuri. File:Haiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden '''Interior of the haiden''' Inside the haiden is a large snake made of {{ill|lt=pinecones|Q118185651|qid=Q118185651|1=pine cone}}, dedicated on {{ill|lt=March 10|Q2397|qid=Q2397|1=March 10}}, {{ill|lt=1932|Q18743|qid=Q18743|1=1932}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 7) by a devotee from {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. In addition, above the connecting section between the honden and heiden are carvings of a man’s and a woman’s face, and above the doors of the honden is displayed an “open-mouthed mask” dedicated in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} ({{ill|lt=1797|Q7820|qid=Q7820|1=1797}}). File:Snake made from pinecones inside of Haiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Large snake made of pinecones inside the haiden
Dedicated in Shōwa 7 by a devotee from Gifu Prefecture. File:Carving of Face of Man of Heiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Carving of a man’s face above the heiden connection File:Carving of face of Woman of Heiden of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Carving of a woman’s face above the heiden connection File:Mask_of_Man's_face_at_Azumayanuma-jinja_shrine.jpg|Male mask dedicated in Kansei 9, displayed above the honden doors === Other === File:Small shrines of Azumayanuma-jija shrine.JPG|Auxiliary shrines File:Precincts of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Shrine grounds File:Torii gate of Azumayanuma-jinja shrine.JPG|Torii gate Behind the honden are stone miniature shrines, and within the grounds are a mikoshi storehouse, the Azumayanuma Shrine Museum, and playground equipment. Immediately behind the shrine buildings is {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q5769419|qid=Q5769419|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Fukushima Transportation Iizaka Line|Q5507732|qid=Q5507732|1=Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line}}. === References === * “Azumayanuma Shrine History” (Azumayanuma Shrine, April {{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}} [Shōwa 61]) === Local information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fukushima City|Q161176|qid=Q161176|1=Fukushima (city)}}{{ill|lt=Hirano, Iizaka-machi|Q11666149|qid=Q11666149|1=iizakamachihirano}} Aza Myōjinwaki 1 '''Access''' * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Transportation|Q5507741|qid=Q5507741|1=Fukushima Transportation}}{{ill|lt=Fukushima Transportation Iizaka Line|Q5507732|qid=Q5507732|lt=Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Hirano Station (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q5769419|qid=Q5769419|lt=Hirano Station|1=Hirano Station (Fukushima)}} (a short walk) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276002.html Azumayanuma Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Database”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Azumayanuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azumayanuma Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:福島市の歴史]] [[Category:福島市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]]
